step_by_step_thinking:
Intraoperative histopathological analysis typically provides rapid but not always fully definitive diagnoses due to limitations like fixation artifacts and technical issues during processing. It does help in assessing margin status post-resection and confirming local findings, especially regarding metastases if present. However, sentinel lymph node biopsy in breast carcinoma being exclusively about identifying initial drainage nodes rather than giving real-time intraoperative diagnosis makes option D incorrect compared to others.
\boxed{D} Based on the evidence, intraoperative histopathological analysis primarily gives near-immediate feedback on margin status and local findings but cannot definitively diagnose tumors without some degree of uncertainty.
